query_normalized: unveiling hidden correlations between processing parameters and mechanical properties in additively manufactured alloys materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) as it empirically investigates the correlation between specific material processing parameters (doping, precipitate formation, grain refinement) and mechanical properties (strength, stability) within a specific class of alloys, providing the type of mechanism-based evidence required for a literature review on processing-property relationships in additively manufactured alloys.
